name: Cordon Bleu Chicken Rolls
instructions: 1. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit (200 degrees Celsius) and lightly grease a baking dish. 2. Place each chicken breast half between plastic wrap and pound to 1/8 inch thickness. 3. Place a slice of Swiss cheese on each slice of ham, season with the dried thyme, salt, and pepper, then roll up jellyroll-style. 4. Wrap each chicken breast around the ham and cheese, tuck the ends, and secure with toothpicks. 5. Dip each chicken roll in melted butter, coat with the cornflakes, and place in the baking dish. 6. Bake for about 40 minutes until golden brown and the juices run clear. 7. For the sauce, heat the chicken broth, sour cream, and lemon juice over low heat until combined. 8. Serve the hot sauce over the chicken rolls.
simpleInstructions: 1. Preheat and grease baking dish. 2. Pound the chicken thin, wrap the Swiss cheese in the ham, and roll with the chicken. 3. Coat with the butter and cornflakes, then bake. 4. Make the sauce with the chicken broth, sour cream, and lemon juice, and serve with the chicken rolls.
